apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of symbolism to other poems and explain its significance.
Students will be able to use the poem as an example to explain the role of imagery in poetry.
Students will be able to analyze the poet's use of figurative language to convey the theme of the poem.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the poem's structure and organization and explain its effect on the reader.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the poem with other poems on the same theme.
Students will be able to evaluate the poet's use of language to convey the theme of the poem.
remember: Students will be able to recall and list the major themes and motifs in the poem 'Landscape of the Soul'.
Students will be able to identify and explain the symbolism used in the poem.
Students will be able to describe the poet's tone and mood in the poem.
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of the title 'Landscape of the Soul' and its relation to the poem.
Students will be able to analyze the poet's use of imagery to convey the theme of the poem.
Students will be able to discuss the role of nature in the poem and its significance.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the theme of the poem and its significance in the context of human experience.
As per NCERT objective 2: To analyze the poet's use of imagery and symbolism to convey the theme of the poem.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and explain the major themes and motifs in the poem.
To analyze the poet's use of figurative language to convey the theme of the poem.
To discuss the role of nature in the poem and its significance.
